metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: International Day of Mathematics is celebrated every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Day of Mathematics is celebrated every year on 14 March. The day highlights the importance of mathematics in daily life, science and technology. It has been celebrated globally since 2020 after proclamation by UNESCO. The celebrations are coordinated by International Mathematical Union with support from UNESCO. Schools, universities and research institutions organize competitions, exhibitions and public activities. The aim is to promote interest in mathematics and show its practical role in society and innovation.

questions.question: International Day of Forests is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: International Day of Forests is observed annually on 21 March, proclaimed by the United Nations General Assembly in 2012, to raise awareness about the importance of forests. The theme for 2026 is “Forests and Economies”, highlighting the role of forests in livelihoods, income generation and economic sustainability. Forests are vital ecosystems that help in climate regulation, biodiversity conservation, and supporting communities. More than 1.6 billion people depend on forests for livelihood, and the sector provides 13 million formal jobs and 45–50 million informal jobs globally. Forests provide food, fuel, shelter and income, especially during economic shocks, conflicts and climate crises, but are often underrepresented in economic planning.

questions.question: Earth Hour is organized globally by which organization?

options.label: D
options.text: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)

questions.explanation: Earth Hour is a global grassroots movement to protect the environment and fight climate change. It is organized by World Wildlife Fund (WWF – World Wildlife Fund). In India, awareness activities were held with Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC – Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change). It started in Sydney in 2007 as a lights-off event. It is held at the end of March every year.

questions.question: World Homoeopathy Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: World Homoeopathy Day is observed annually on April 10 to commemorate the birth anniversary of Samuel Hahnemann. The theme for 2026 is “Homoeopathy for Sustainable Health.” India has one of the largest homoeopathic workforces in the world. Homoeopathy was introduced in India in 1810 by German missionaries distributing medicines. India currently has about 3.45 lakh registered homoeopathy doctors, 8,593 dispensaries, 277 educational institutions, and 34 research centres.

questions.question: World Liver Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: World Liver Day is observed annually on 19 April to raise awareness about liver diseases and their prevention. The liver is the second-largest organ and performs vital functions such as metabolism, detoxification, digestion, immunity, and nutrient storage. Despite its ability to regenerate up to 60–70%, liver damage can lead to serious conditions like cirrhosis and cancer. Globally, about 2 million deaths occur annually due to liver diseases, including cirrhosis, viral hepatitis, and liver cancer. The theme for 2026 is “Solid Habits, Strong Liver,” emphasising healthy lifestyle, balanced diet, exercise, and regular health check-ups.

questions.question: National Panchayati Raj Day (NPRD) is celebrated on which day every year?
questions.explanation: National Panchayati Raj Day (NPRD) is celebrated on 24 April every year to mark the enactment of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, which gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs). In 2026, it marks 33 years of strengthening grassroots democracy in India. The main event is organized by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj at Vigyan Bhawan. The day highlights the role of the three-tier Panchayati Raj system in achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat.

questions.question: World Book and Copyright Day is observed on which day every year?
questions.explanation: World Book and Copyright Day is observed on 23 April every year to promote reading, books, and copyright awareness worldwide. The date is linked to literary figures like William Shakespeare and Miguel de Cervantes. It was officially declared by UNESCO in 1995. Rabat is the World Book Capital for 2026, promoting literacy, multilingualism, and access to books. The day highlights the importance of reading culture, cultural exchange, and protection of authors’ rights in a knowledge-based society.

questions.question: What is the theme of Mehar Baba Competition-3 launched by Indian Air Force?
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force launched Mehar Baba Competition-3 (MBC-3) on 27 April 2026 to promote drone-based radar innovation. It is the third edition of the competition, originally started in 2018, to encourage defence technology development. The theme is “Collaborative Drone-Based Surveillance Radars.” The aim is to develop a swarm of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) acting as airborne radar for detecting and tracking aerial targets. The competition is named after Mehar Singh, a Maha Vir Chakra awardee and pioneering air veteran.

questions.question: International Labour Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Labour Day, also called International Workers’ Day, is celebrated every year on May 1 to honour the hard work and contributions of workers across all sectors. The day highlights the need for fair wages, safe working conditions, and reasonable working hours for all employees. It also reminds us of the struggle of workers in history and the continued importance of protecting their rights. The International Labour Organization (ILO), a United Nations agency, plays a key role in setting global labour standards.

questions.question: International Scurvy Awareness Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: International Scurvy Awareness Day is observed annually on May 2 to spread awareness about Vitamin C deficiency. Scurvy is caused by a lack of Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) in the diet. The day highlights that scurvy is not just a historical disease but still occurs today due to poor nutrition. It mainly affects people with limited or unbalanced diets, including the elderly and picky eaters. The disease can be prevented by consuming Vitamin C-rich foods like citrus fruits, guava, tomatoes, and green vegetables. Scurvy is fully preventable and treatable through proper diet and supplementation. The day promotes better nutrition and awareness for overall public health.
